---
title: "Rugby Affective Visualization"
author: "Leah Pettway"
date: "03/12/26"
format: html
---

## Initial Set-up
```{r}
#| label: Set-up

library(tidyverse) #loading in initial packages necessary for visualizations
library(janitor)
library(grid)
library(ggridges)
library(GGally)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(plotly)
library(heatmaply)
library(showtext)
library(sysfonts)
 # installing a color package
library(wesanderson)
library(ggcorrplot) 
library(ggrugby) # loading in ggrugby package for field shape
showtext.auto()
my_data <- read_csv("/Users/leahpettway/ENVS-193DS/github/affective_visualization/Rugby-Affective-Visualization/data/Personal_data.csv") # loading in personal data 

```

## Cleaning Data

```{r}
#| label: cleaning personal data


set.seed(123) # ensuring the same output of numbers occurs each time the code is ran.

# Clean data and create formation
my_data_pitch <- my_data |>
  clean_names() |> # cleaning names so they are all lowercase and remove spaces
  drop_na() |> # dropping any n/a values in the data
  mutate(
    # spread players across pitch width
    width_position = seq(5, 65, length.out = n()),
    
    # formation positions
    x_position = ifelse(
      rugby_practice_day_yes_or_no == "yes",
      # attacking backline behind right 10m line
      58 + 8 * sin(width_position / 10),
      
      # defensive line behind left 10m line
      42
    ),
    y_position = width_position 
  )
my_data_clean <- my_data |> # create clean data object for other visualizations
  clean_names() |> # cleaning names so they are all lowercase and remove spaces
  drop_na() # dropping n/a values from the data set


```

## 1. Rugby Pitch scatterplot

```{r}
#| label: Rugby Plot

font_add_google("Playfair Display", "wesfont")
showtext_auto()


line_arrows <- data.frame(
  x = c(75, 20),        # where the arrow starts (pointing at the line)
  y = c(55, 35),
  xend = c(55, 35),     # where the arrow points
  yend = c(40, 30),
  label = c("Attack Line", "Defense Line")  # text to display
)

t
ggplot() +
  rugby_pitch() +

  # Protein intake points
  geom_point(
    data = my_data_pitch,
    aes(
      x = x_position,
      y = y_position,
      size = amount_of_protein_grams,
      color = rugby_practice_day_yes_or_no
    ),
    alpha = 0.85,
    shape = 4,
    stroke = 0.8
  ) +

  # Arrows pointing at attack and defense lines
  geom_curve(
    data = line_arrows,
    aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),
    curvature = 0.2,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.03, "npc")),
    color = "#2E4053",
    size = 0.8
  ) +

  # Labels for attack/defense lines
  geom_text(
    data = line_arrows,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
    family = "wesfont",
    color = "#2E4053",
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold",
    nudge_x = -2,
    nudge_y = 2
  ) +

  # Color and size scales for protein points
  scale_color_manual(values = c("yes" = "#8B0000", "no" = "#404472")) +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 8)) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.05)) +

  coord_fixed() +

  # Titles and labels
  labs(
    title = "Protein Intake on Rugby vs Non-Rugby Days",
    color = "Practice Day",
    size = "Protein Intake (g)"
  ) +

  # Wes Anderson theme
  theme_void(base_family = "wesfont") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 20, color = "#2E4053"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(face = "italic", size = 14, color = "#566573"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
  )
```


## 2. Creating a correlation heatmap
```{r}
#| label: Correlation map

# Font
font_add_google("Playfair Display", "wesfont")

# Wes Anderson palette
wes_colors <- wes_palette("AsteroidCity1", 3)
wes_gradient <- colorRampPalette(wes_colors)(100)

heat_data <- my_data_clean |> 
  select(
    amount_of_protein_grams,
    total_caloric_count_of_protein,
    caffeine_intake_milligrams,
    number_of_calories_burned_calories
  ) |> 
  rename(
    "Protein (g)" = amount_of_protein_grams,
    "Protein Calories" = total_caloric_count_of_protein,
    "Caffeine (mg)" = caffeine_intake_milligrams,
    "Calories Burned" = number_of_calories_burned_calories
  )

# Correlation matrix
matrix <- cor(heat_data, use = "complete.obs")

heatmaply(
  matrix,
  colors = wes_gradient,        # center scale at 0
  dendrogram = "none",
  xlab = "Variables",
  ylab = "Variables",
  main = "Correlation Heatmap of Personal Nutrition & Activity Data",
  scale = "none",
  margins = c(80, 200, 50, 20),  # pushes y labels left
  grid_color = "white",
  hide_colorbar = FALSE,
  fontsize_row = 12,
  fontsize_col = 12,
  cellnote = round(matrix,2),
  notecex = 1
) |> 
layout(
  font = list(family = "Playfair Display"),
  yaxis = list(
    title = list(
      text = "Variables",
      standoff = 10   # pushes title away from labels
    )
  )
)

```

## 3. Creating a ridge line plot

```{r}
#| label: Ridge Plot

font_add_google("Playfair Display", "wesfont")
showtext_auto()

wes_colors <- c("yes" = "#8B0000",  
                "no"  = "#404472")  

ggplot(my_data_clean,
       aes(x = amount_of_protein_grams,
           y = rugby_practice_day_yes_or_no,
           fill = rugby_practice_day_yes_or_no)) +
  
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.7, scale = 1.2, color = "#2E4053", size = 0.3) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = wes_colors) +
  
  labs(
    x = "Protein Intake (g)",
    y = "Rugby Practice Day",
    title = "Distribution of Protein Intake on Rugby vs Non-Rugby Days"
  ) +
  
  theme_ridges(font_family = "wesfont") +  # sets font for the plot
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18, color = "#2E4053", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10, color = "#566573"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```


## E. Write up

####
